title: Velocity
short: How fast an object moves and in which direction, measured in m/s. Speed is its magnitude.
full:
Velocity is the rate of change of position, v = Δx / Δt. It is a vector — it carries a direction — whereas speed is just its magnitude. Walking 2 m/s east and 2 m/s west are the same speed but opposite velocities.

Under constant acceleration the velocity changes uniformly, v = v₀ + a·t, where v₀ is the initial velocity.

The SI unit is metres per second (m/s); 1 m/s is 3.6 km/h.
